scenario: A team's IDP handles invoices in PDF format primarily. Customers occasionally send invoices as images (PNG / JPG) or Word documents (DOCX). The team wonders which formats IDP supports.
question: Which document formats does Camunda IDP support?

options.text: Verify per Camunda IDP version + underlying engine: typically supports common business document formats: - PDF: most common; extraction engines mature. - Images (PNG, JPG, TIFF): OCR-based extraction; quality depends on resolution / clarity. - Word / DOCX: text + structure extraction. - Email (MSG / EML): some IDP versions support email parsing. , HEIC images → JPG) before IDP. Quality of extraction varies by format / source quality (scanned vs digital).

Camunda IDP only supports PDF documents and rejects all other formats -- partial; multiple document formats are typically supported depending on the IDP version and underlying extraction engine. PDF is the most mature and common format for business documents, but IDP implementations generally handle images (PNG, JPG, TIFF) via OCR, Word documents (DOCX) with text and table extraction, and in some versions email formats (MSG, EML). Restricting to PDF only would exclude a significant portion of real-world business documents that arrive as scans, photos, or office files.

IDP accepts any document format without restrictions -- overstates; some formats require pre-processing conversion before IDP can extract data reliably. Uncommon or legacy formats like HEIC images (Apple format), old binary DOC files, and archive formats like ZIP containing multiple documents typically need conversion to supported formats first. The quality of extraction also varies significantly: digital PDFs yield excellent results while low-resolution scanned images may produce poor OCR accuracy. Teams should validate supported formats against their specific Camunda IDP version and plan conversion pipelines for edge cases.

Camunda IDP does not support document processing and serves a different purpose -- wrong; Intelligent Document Processing is precisely Camunda's feature for extracting structured data from unstructured documents. IDP is specifically designed to intake business documents (invoices, contracts, claims forms), apply AI-based extraction to identify and extract fields like vendor name, total amount, and due date, and feed the extracted data into BPMN process variables. This eliminates manual data entry bottlenecks in document-intensive workflows. Documents are the primary use case that IDP was built to address.

a.text: Correct. Camunda IDP's supported formats depend on: Camunda 8 version: IDP features evolve. Underlying extraction engine: IDP may use specific OCR / ML services with their own format support. Typically supported: PDF: digital (text-based) and scanned (image-based with OCR). Images: PNG, JPG, TIFF, etc. for scanned documents. Word / DOCX: text + tables. Email (MSG, EML): in some versions. Plain text: structured / unstructured. Less commonly supported (may need conversion): HEIC (Apple image): convert to JPG first. Legacy formats (DOC, very old): convert to modern equivalents. Archives (ZIP with multiple docs): extract individual files first. Pre-processing pipeline: Convert non-supported to supported formats. Pre-OCR optimisation: improve image quality (rotate, de-skew, contrast) for better extraction. Split multi-page documents: if IDP needs per-page processing. Quality factors: Digital documents (PDF with embedded text): high extraction quality. Scanned documents (image-PDFs, photos): OCR-dependent; quality varies with resolution / clarity / language. Handwriting: typically harder; some engines support; quality variable. Verify your Camunda IDP version's exact format support + best practices for input quality.
